# UC-10.12.36 · Inventory System Sync Failures

## Description

OMS/IMS drift causes oversell, BOPIS failures, and poor customer experience.

## Value

OMS/IMS drift causes oversell, BOPIS failures, and poor customer experience.

## Implementation

Dead-letter queue depth alerts; reconcile counts nightly; prioritize high-velocity SKUs.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: WMS, e-commerce OMS.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="ims:sync"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Dead-letter queue depth alerts; reconcile counts nightly; prioritize high-velocity SKUs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=retail sourcetype="ims:sync"
| search status IN ("FAIL","TIMEOUT","PARTIAL")
| stats count, latest(error_code) as err by sku, channel, dc_id
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Inventory System Sync Failures** — OMS/IMS drift causes oversell, BOPIS failures, and poor customer experience.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="ims:sync"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): WMS, e-commerce OMS. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: retail; **sourcetype**: ims:sync. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=retail, sourcetype="ims:sync". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sku, channel, dc_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed SKUs), Line chart (sync lag), Single value (unresolved errors).

## SPL

```spl
index=retail sourcetype="ims:sync"
| search status IN ("FAIL","TIMEOUT","PARTIAL")
| stats count, latest(error_code) as err by sku, channel, dc_id
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (failed SKUs), Line chart (sync lag), Single value (unresolved errors).
